text: Meeting deadlines is an important skill in both school and work. Good time management helps you break large tasks into smaller steps and set daily goals. Prioritization means deciding which tasks are most urgent or important, so you focus on those first. Scope management involves knowing what you can realistically achieve and avoiding adding extra work without adjusting the timeline. Finally, communication is key: if you foresee a problem, inform your team or supervisor early. Asking for help or renegotiating deadlines shows responsibility. By combining these skills, you can handle deadlines with less stress and more success.
